sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER.
Questions 11-16: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 17-20: Choose FOUR answers from the list below and write the correct letter A-F next to questions 17-20.

questions.content: According to the speaker, in what way is Camber’s different from other theme parks?
options: A it is suitable for different age groups
B it offers lots to do in wet weather
C it has a focus on education

questions.content: What is included in the entrance fee?
options: A most rides and parking
B all rides and some exhibits
C parking and all rides

questions.content: Becoming a member of the Adventurers Club means
options: A you can avoid queuing so much
B you can enter the park free for a year
C you can visit certain zones closed to other people

questions.content: The Future Farm zone encourages visitors to
options: A buy animals as pets
B learn about the care of animals
C get close to the animals

questions.content: When is hot food available in the park?
options: A 10 am – 5.30 pm
B 11 am – 5 pm
C 10.30 am – 5 pm

questions.content:
(17) River Adventure
(18) Jungle Jim Rollercoaster
(19) Swoop Slide
(20) Zip Go-carts
options: A Must be over a certain age
B Must use special safety equipment
C Must avoid it if they have health problems
D Must wear a particular type of clothing
E Must be over a certain height
F Must be accompanied by an adult if under 16

sections.instruction: Questions 21-26: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 27-30: Choose FOUR answers from the list below and write the correct letter A-F next to questions 27-30.

questions.content: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree to say about listening in groups?
options: A Listening skills are often overlooked in business training
B Learning to listen well is a skill that is easy for most people to learn
C It is sometimes acceptable to argue against speakers
D Body language is very important when listening
E Listeners should avoid interrupting speakers

questions.content: What TWO things does the article say about goal setting?
options: A Meetings should start with a clear statement of goals
B It is important for each individual’ goals to be explained
C Everybody in the group should have the same goals
D Goals should be a mix of the realistic and the ideal
E Goals must always to be achievable within a set time

questions.content: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree are weak points in the article’s section on conflict resolution?
options: A It does not explore the topic in enough detail
B It only discusses conservative views
C It says nothing about the potential value of conflict
D It talks too much about winners and losers
E It does not provide definitions of key terms

questions.content:
(27) Preparing the powerpoint
(28) Using direct quotations
(29) Creating a handout
(30) Drawing up a bibliography
options: A Contact the tutor for clarification
B Check the assignment specifications
C Leave it until the last task
D Ask a course-mate to help
E Find information on the internet
F Look through course handbooks
